channel.title: Legals on 112 Revelats
channel.description: Recent content in Legals on 112 Revelats

item.title: Collaborate with 112 Revelats
item.description:
Collaborate with 112 Revelats Current challenge: Slow Portraits

112 Revelats is a collective editorial project powered by 112 Books. Companies and organizations related to photography can contribute financially or in-kind to help fund the printed edition, design, production and dissemination of the project.

Sponsors receive recognition and visibility according to their contribution level.

Personal details

Type of participation

Visibility & recognition

I authorize the display of our name (company or individual) on the 112 Revelats website:

item.title: Participation form — Slow Portraits
item.description:
112 Revelats – Participation form Current challenge: Slow Portraits

Submission deadline: September 6, 2026

Some images need time. A gaze that builds while the shutter stays open. Light passing through a tiny hole, projecting the world upside down inside a can. In a moment where everything moves fast, we propose returning to the pause: building a portrait from waiting, observation, and long exposure.

This call is for photographers who want to explore slow portraiture: pinhole cameras, long exposure, slow photography. No professional equipment needed. A tin can, photographic paper, and patience are enough.

item.title: Terms & Conditions — Slow Portraits
item.description:
112 Revelats – Participation Terms Current challenge: Slow Portraits Submission deadline: September 6, 2026

1. Project purpose

The 112 Revelats project, driven by the publisher 112 Books, aims to collect original photographic works to form part of a collective publication. This initiative seeks to give visibility to emerging and established photographers through digital publications and, if possible, printed editions, as well as presentations, exhibitions and other associated cultural activities.

2. Participation requirements

Photographers over 18 years of age, of any nationality or residence, may participate. Participants must fully accept these terms. Only original works created by the author will be accepted; AI-generated images or plagiarized works are not allowed. Proposals must be submitted within the period established by the organization.
